rationale: The citing paper uses Aaker and Jacobson (2001) in its core financial-market valuation framing, grouping it with prior studies that link customer mindset brand measures to stock returns and show long-term financial implications beyond current profits. It then explicitly argues that these prior studies did not quantify total impact or impact dynamics, which is presented as the gap the 2014 paper addresses. So the citation is not a passing mention; it helps define the prior state of evidence and motivates the new model and contribution.
relation_description: The 2014 paper extends the line of work represented by Aaker and Jacobson (2001): it moves from showing incremental value relevance of brand attitude to estimating total financial impact and decomposing contemporaneous versus future effects with limited time-series data. It broadens that stock-return based branding approach across industries and emphasizes heterogeneity in impact dynamics.
